rationale: I split Electrical & Electronic Engineering into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to cover major research and engineering communities (power, communications, IC design, signal/control, photonics, sensors, electromagnetics, instrumentation, etc.) while keeping each card large enough for discoverability. I intentionally kept IC design separate from Analog/RF and from Embedded Systems because those are distinct career identities and publication venues; likewise Communications and Signal Processing are split since many papers are clearly one or the other. I avoided slicing emerging niches (e.g., neuromorphic hardware, computational electromagnetics) into standalone cards to prevent sub-0.5% fragments — those remain under broader categories like Semiconductor Devices, Signal Processing, or Electromagnetics depending on emphasis.

subspecialties.name: Communications & Networking
subspecialties.tagline: Wireless, wired comms, information theory, network protocols
covers: Physical-layer and system-level communications research
Examples: 5G/6G wireless, optical communications, network protocols, information theory
explicitly_excludes: Signal Processing (algorithms like denoising, source separation)
Photonics & Optoelectronics (device/laser physics for photonic components)

subspecialties.name: Signal Processing
subspecialties.tagline: Estimation, transforms, filtering, time‑series and image processing
covers: Theory and algorithms for processing signals and images
Examples: adaptive filtering, compressed sensing, spectral analysis, audio/vision signal algorithms
explicitly_excludes: Communications & Networking (system/protocol-level comms papers)
Machine Learning / Algorithms (goes to Computer Science & AI)

subspecialties.name: Control Systems & Robotics
subspecialties.tagline: Control theory, estimation, motion planning, embedded robot control
covers: Feedback control, state estimation, robotics hardware control
Examples: nonlinear control, model predictive control, robot manipulators, sensor fusion for autonomy
explicitly_excludes: Robotics perception algorithms that are primarily ML → Computer Science & AI
Embedded Systems (hardware/firmware-centric implementation details)

subspecialties.name: Power Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Generation, transmission, distribution, stability and grid integration
covers: Large‑scale electric power engineering and grid studies
Examples: stability analysis, renewable integration, smart grid control, distribution planning
explicitly_excludes: Power Electronics (converter/drive hardware design)
Energy materials or fabrication → Materials Science

subspecialties.name: Power Electronics & Motor Drives
subspecialties.tagline: Converters, inverters, motor control and high‑power electronics
covers: Design and control of power converters and electric drives
Examples: DC–DC/AC converters, EV inverters, wide-bandgap devices in converters
explicitly_excludes: Power Systems (system-level grid studies)
Semiconductor device physics and fabrication → Semiconductor Devices or Materials Science

subspecialties.name: Analog & RF Circuits
subspecialties.tagline: Low‑frequency analog, RF front‑ends, amplifiers, mixers, PLLs
covers: Circuit design for analog and radio-frequency applications
Examples: low‑noise amplifiers, ADC/DAC front-ends, RF transceiver front-ends, PLL design
explicitly_excludes: Digital & Mixed‑Signal IC (digital-heavy chip design)
Antenna-focused electromagnetics → Electromagnetics & Antennas

subspecialties.name: Digital & Mixed-Signal IC
subspecialties.tagline: Digital chip architecture, mixed‑signal SoCs, VLSI design
covers: Integrated circuit design and on‑chip systems combining digital and analog
Examples: microarchitecture, low‑power CMOS design, mixed‑signal SoCs, timing/physical design
explicitly_excludes: Device physics and fabrication → Materials Science
Embedded systems software and board-level integration → Embedded Systems & Microcontrollers

subspecialties.name: Embedded Systems & Microcontrollers
subspecialties.tagline: Firmware, real‑time systems, sensor nodes, hardware–software co‑design
covers: Design and integration of embedded hardware and firmware
Examples: real‑time OS, low‑power sensor nodes, SoC integration, hardware–software co‑design
explicitly_excludes: VLSI/IC design (chip‑level design) → Digital & Mixed‑Signal IC
Control theory research without embedded implementation → Control Systems & Robotics

subspecialties.name: Photonics & Optoelectronics
subspecialties.tagline: Lasers, optical systems, integrated photonics, detectors
covers: Optical devices and systems, photonic integration and optoelectronic components
Examples: photonic integrated circuits, modulators, lasers, optical sensing systems
explicitly_excludes: Optical communications system-level networking → Communications & Networking
Optical materials/fabrication-focused work → Materials Science

subspecialties.name: Semiconductor Devices
subspecialties.tagline: Device physics, transistors, sensors as devices (not fabs)
covers: Physics, modeling and device-level design of semiconductors and sensors
Examples: transistor device physics, reliability, device modeling, MEMS device behavior
explicitly_excludes: Device fabrication and process engineering → Materials Science
System-level IC design → Digital & Mixed‑Signal IC

subspecialties.name: Sensors & MEMS
subspecialties.tagline: Sensing technologies, MEMS devices, transducers and front‑ends
covers: Design and application of sensors and microelectromechanical systems
Examples: inertial sensors, chemical/biomedical sensors, MEMS resonators, sensor interfaces
explicitly_excludes: MEMS fabrication/process work → Materials Science
Signal‑processing algorithms for sensor data → Signal Processing

subspecialties.name: Electromagnetics & Antennas
subspecialties.tagline: Wave propagation, antennas, scattering, microwave engineering
covers: Electromagnetic theory and applied antenna/microwave engineering
Examples: antenna design, radar systems, scattering theory, metamaterials (if EM‑focused)
explicitly_excludes: RF circuit design (goes to Analog & RF Circuits)
Metamaterials focused on fabrication/materials → Materials Science

subspecialties.name: Instrumentation & Measurement
subspecialties.tagline: Sensors, test equipment, metrology, data acquisition systems
covers: Design of measurement systems, testbeds and metrology techniques
Examples: precision instrumentation, sensor calibration, data-acquisition systems and test rigs
explicitly_excludes: Signal-processing algorithms for measured data → Signal Processing
Fabrication of measurement devices → Materials Science

subspecialties.name: Bioelectronics
subspecialties.tagline: Electronics for biomedical sensing, neural interfaces, medical devices
covers: Electronic design and systems for biological/medical applications
Examples: neural implants, wearable bio‑sensors, electrode interfaces, implantable electronics
explicitly_excludes: Biomedical algorithms and clinical studies → Biomedical / Life Sciences
Biomaterials and device fabrication → Materials Science

uncovered_concerns: Neuromorphic hardware and AI accelerators: considered but folded into Semiconductor Devices or Digital & Mixed‑Signal IC because standalone audience is small.
Metamaterials and novel EM materials: often straddle Electromagnetics and Materials Science; sent to Materials Science when fabrication/material focus dominates.
Quantum hardware: some EE groups work on it, but it's dispersed across Photonics, Semiconductor Devices, and Quantum-specific venues (decided not to add a separate small card).
Machine learning algorithmic work: explicitly excluded and directed to Computer Science & AI to avoid overlap.
